question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here's the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` for a Kubernetes charm:

## Required Fields

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `type` | Always `charm` |
| `name` | Charm identifier (convention: `<workload>-k8s` for K8s charms) |
| `title` | Human-readable name |
| `summary` | One-line description (max 78 chars) |
| `description` | Extended description for Charmhub |
| `base` | Runtime OS, e.g. `ubuntu@24.04` |
| `platforms` | Build/deployment architecture mapping |
| `containers` | Workload container definitions (required for K8s charms) |

## Complete Example

```yaml
type: charm
name: myapp-k8s
title: My Application
summary: A Kubernetes charm for My Application
description: |
  This charm deploys and manages My Application on Kubernetes
  using Juju and Pebble.

base: ubuntu@24.04
platforms:
  amd64:
    build-on: [amd64]
    build-for: [amd64]
  arm64:
    build-on: [amd64, arm64]
    build-for: [arm64]

assumes:
  - k8s-api
  - juju >= 3.5

charm-user: sudoer

# Workload containers (required for K8s charms)
containers:
  myapp:
    resource: myapp-image
    mounts:
      - storage: data
        location: /var/lib/myapp

# OCI image resources (one per container)
resources:
  myapp-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for My Application

# Persistent storage
storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    location: /var/lib/myapp
    description: Application data

# User-facing configuration
config:
  options:
    port:
      type: int
      default: 8080
      description: HTTP port for the application
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: info
      description: "Logging level (debug, info, warning, error)"

# Actions
actions:
  restart:
    description: Restart the application workload

# Integration endpoints
provides:
  metrics-endpoint:
    interface: prometheus_scrape
    optional: true

requires:
  database:
    interface: postgresql_client
    optional: false
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    optional: true

peers:
  cluster:
    interface: myapp-cluster

# Charm library dependencies
charm-libs:
  - lib: traefik_k8s.ingress
    version: "2"

# Project links
links:
  documentation: https://example.com/docs
  source: https://github.com/example/myapp-k8s-operator
  issues: https://github.com/example/myapp-k8s-operator/issues

# Build configuration
parts:
  my-charm:
    plugin: charm
    source: .
    charm-entrypoint: src/charm.py
    charm-requirements: [requirements.txt]
```

## Key points

- **`containers`** is what makes it a Kubernetes charm — each container needs a matching `oci-image` resource.
- **`assumes: [k8s-api]`** declares that the charm requires a Kubernetes model.
- Per CLAUDE.md guidelines, always include the `optional` key on relation endpoints.
- The `parts` section controls the build process — `plugin: charm` is standard.
- `platforms` maps architectures; use shorthand `amd64:` (without sub-keys) if build-on and build-for are the same.
